result:
## Summary

The issue was that AnkiDroid couldn't see or open CSV files when they had the standard `text/csv` MIME type. Two changes were needed:

### Changes Made

**1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt:338`**

Added `text/csv` to the MIME type check in `getLaunchType()`. Previously, only `text/tab-separated-values` and `text/comma-separated-values` were recognized as text import types. The standard IANA MIME type for CSV files is `text/csv`, which Android's `MimeTypeMap` uses when resolving `.csv` file extensions. Without this, CSV files opened from external apps (file managers, Google Drive, etc.) would fall through to the `FILE_IMPORT` path (APKG/COLPKG handler) and fail.

**2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml:244`**

Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the MIME-type-based intent filter. This ensures AnkiDroid appears in the Android "Open with" chooser when a CSV file with `text/csv` MIME type is shared from another app. The existing filter only declared `text/comma-separated-values` and `text/tab-separated-values`, missing the standard `text/csv` type that most file managers and apps use.
